name: config
context: Create and configure vite.config.ts/js with defineConfig, conditional config, resolve.alias, CSS modules, preprocessors, and shared options
info: This skill covers creating and configuring the Vite configuration file (vite.config.ts, vite.config.js, vite.config.mts, or vite.config.mjs). The defineConfig helper provides type-safe IntelliSense without manual type annotations. Config can be a static object or a function that receives { command, mode, isSsrBuild, isPreview } for conditional logic (e.g., different plugins for dev vs build). Async config is supported â€” return a promise or use top-level await for dynamic imports. The loadEnv(mode, envDir, prefixes?) helper reads .env files into a record, useful when config itself needs env values (import.meta.env is not available in config). Shared options include root (project root), base (public base path for deployment), mode (development/production/custom), define (global constant replacements at build time), plugins (array of Vite/Rollup plugins â€” see [[vite/plugins]]), publicDir (static assets copied as-is), cacheDir (dependency pre-bundle cache), resolve.alias (path mapping like @ -> ./src, compatible with [[typescript/config]] paths), resolve.extensions (import resolution order), css.modules (CSS Modules scoping and naming), css.preprocessorOptions (Sass/Less/Stylus global config), css.postcss (inline PostCSS config or path), css.lightningcss (Lightning CSS options), json.stringify (performance optimization), esbuild/oxc (transpiler options), assetsInclude (extra asset file extensions), logLevel (info/warn/error/silent), envDir (.env file directory), envPrefix (client-exposed env prefix, default VITE_), and appType (spa/mpa/custom). Server options are covered in [[vite/dev-server]], build options in [[vite/build]], preview in [[vite/dev-server]], SSR config in [[vite/build]], and environment variables in [[vite/env]].
triggers: set up resolve alias in vite
conditional vite config for dev and build
set up vite config from scratch

sections.title: Basic config with defineConfig
sections.body: Create `vite.config.ts` at the project root and export a config object wrapped in `defineConfig` for type safety. Import and add the appropriate framework plugin to the `plugins` array: `@vitejs/plugin-react` for React, `@vitejs/plugin-vue` for Vue, `@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte` for Svelte, `vite-plugin-solid` for Solid. The config file uses native ESM â€” ensure `"type": "module"` is set in package.json or use `.mts`/`.mjs` extension. See [[vite/plugins]] for all framework plugins.

sections.title: Conditional config (dev vs build)
sections.body: Pass a function to `defineConfig` that receives `{ command, mode, isSsrBuild, isPreview }`. `command` is `'serve'` during development and `'build'` during production. `mode` defaults to `'development'` or `'production'` but can be overridden with `--mode staging`. Use this to apply different plugins, define values, or server settings per environment. The function can be async for dynamic imports or remote config fetching.

sections.title: resolve.alias for path mapping
sections.body: Map import aliases like `@` to `./src` using `resolve.alias`. Values must be absolute paths â€” use `path.resolve(__dirname, 'src')` or `new URL('./src', import.meta.url).pathname`. For TypeScript, mirror aliases in `tsconfig.json` `compilerOptions.paths` (see [[typescript/config]]) or install `vite-tsconfig-paths` to read them automatically. Common pattern: `@` -> `src`, `@components` -> `src/components`, `@utils` -> `src/utils`. The `resolve.extensions` array controls which extensions are tried during resolution; the default covers `.mjs`, `.js`, `.mts`, `.ts`, `.jsx`, `.tsx`, `.json`.

sections.title: CSS configuration: modules, preprocessors, PostCSS
sections.body: Configure CSS Modules via `css.modules` â€” set `localsConvention` to `'camelCaseOnly'` for cleaner imports, customize `generateScopedName` for readable class names in dev. Activate CSS Modules by naming files `.module.css` (or `.module.scss`/`.module.less`). Configure preprocessors via `css.preprocessorOptions`: inject global Sass variables with `additionalData`, set Less `globalVars`, or configure Stylus `define`. Install preprocessors separately (`npm i -D sass`). Override `postcss.config.js` with `css.postcss` for inline PostCSS configuration. Lightning CSS (`css.lightningcss`) is an alternative to PostCSS for transforms and minification.

sections.title: define â€” global constant replacement
sections.body: The `define` option replaces global identifiers with constant values at build time. String replacements must be wrapped in `JSON.stringify()` so the output includes quotes. Common uses: `__APP_VERSION__` for package version, `__DEV__` for development-only code, `__BUILD_TIME__` for build timestamps. For TypeScript, declare these globals in a `.d.ts` file (e.g., `src/env.d.ts`) so the compiler recognizes them. During development, values are injected as-is; during build, they are statically replaced and dead-code eliminated.

sections.title: loadEnv for env vars in config
sections.body: Import `loadEnv` from `vite` to read `.env` files inside the config. Call `loadEnv(mode, envDir, prefixes?)` where `mode` matches the current Vite mode, `envDir` is the directory containing `.env` files (usually `process.cwd()`), and `prefixes` controls which env vars are loaded (default: `'VITE_'`, pass `''` for all vars). This is necessary because `import.meta.env` is not available during config evaluation. Common use: reading `API_URL` from `.env` to configure `server.proxy`, or reading `APP_TITLE` for `define` replacements. See [[vite/env]] for `.env` file naming and precedence.

sections.title: Shared options reference
sections.body: Top-level options that affect both dev and build: `root` (project root, default `process.cwd()`), `base` (public base path for CDN/subpath deployment), `mode` (env mode override), `plugins` (array of Vite/Rollup plugins), `publicDir` (static assets directory, default `'public'`), `cacheDir` (pre-bundle cache, default `'node_modules/.vite'`), `resolve` (alias, extensions, conditions, mainFields), `css` (modules, preprocessorOptions, postcss, lightningcss), `json` (namedExports, stringify), `esbuild`/`oxc` (transpiler options), `assetsInclude` (extra asset patterns), `logLevel`, `envDir`, `envPrefix`, `appType`. Server options are in [[vite/dev-server]], build options in [[vite/build]].

scripts.name: Initialize Vite Config
scripts.description: Create a vite.config.ts (or .js) with defineConfig, framework plugin, and resolve.alias for the given framework

FRAMEWORK.description: Framework: react, vue, svelte, solid, or vanilla (default: vanilla)

USE_TYPESCRIPT.description: Whether to use TypeScript config (default: true)

typescript.description: Use TypeScript for the config file

cssModules.description: Enable CSS Modules configuration with camelCase convention

configPath.description: Absolute path to the generated vite.config.ts or vite.config.js

depends.reason: Vite must be installed in the project

author: dojo-community
